description: Soup: the meal made by warm water and patience, holding whatever the day had to offer. Built on the 'su' of 'sulae' (warm). It is the meal that stretches: one more arrival, a little more water.
sound_symbolism: 'su' carries the warmth of 'sulae', 'lo' rounds it, and 'pa' is the pot it sits in.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'sulopa sulae nai.' (The soup is warm.) 'mia sulopa pharelo.' (I cook soup.)

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The pot over the fire fed the household from whatever there was, and its answer to a guest was always yes. Most of the world's comfort food started as this.
